name: Komi
notes: Komi (коми кыв), a Permic (Uralic) language of the Komi Republic, Russia; the Komi-Zyrian literary standard. INPUT CONTRACT: modern Komi Cyrillic text, Unicode NFC, lower-cased by the caller if case-folding is desired. Orthography: the Russian alphabet plus ⟨І і⟩ ('hard i') and ⟨Ӧ ӧ⟩ /ɘ/. Consonant palatalisation is not written with a separate series of letters: ⟨д з л н с т⟩ are realised as /ɟ ʑ ʎ ɲ ɕ c/ before ⟨е ё и ю я⟩ and before the soft sign ⟨ь⟩, so those sequences are entered here as multigraph keys (де, дё, ди, дю, дя, дь, …) which the maximal-munch matcher prefers over the bare letters. ⟨і⟩ is the 'hard i': it spells /i/ after т д с з н л without palatalising them, so it is deliberately NOT part of any digraph. The affricates /dʒ dʑ tʃ/ are written with the digraphs ⟨дж дз тш⟩, also entered as keys. Iotated letters ⟨е ё ю я⟩ list the plain vowel first and the [j]-initial reading second (the latter applies word-initially and after a vowel); the engine is context-free, so the alternative is offered as a second candidate rather than selected. ⟨ъ⟩ maps to the empty string. Historic Old Permic (Abur) and Molodtsov orthographies are out of scope: input is assumed to be the 1938-onward Cyrillic orthography.

sources.notes: Letter-by-letter Cyrillic→IPA table (including і, ӧ and the digraphs дж /dʒ/, дз /dʑ/, тш /tʃ/) and the rule that д з л н с т are palatalised to ɟ ʑ ʎ ɲ ɕ c before е ё и ю я ь; consonant and vowel phoneme inventories. Source of every value in this spec.

ancestors.notes: Uralic — the classification node this language sits under.

location.notes: Glottolog's computed centroid for the Komi family — a summary of a whole clade's range, not a place anyone speaks.
